css: Canvas: --mm-canvas-base: #F1EBDD (warm ivory parchment, never flat white)
Ink primary: --mm-ink-primary: #2E2923 (warm charcoal, never pure black)
Shadow: warm only — rgba(57,42,24,0.05–0.09), never cool gray
Dark mode: #0B0D10 canvas, glassmorphism enabled (blur 18px), coral accent #FF5A5F
brandGuidelines: Light mode: parchment intelligence — warm archival canvas, technical linework, vellum surfaces, quietly cinematic
Dark mode: KNYT Glass — near-black cinematic, glassmorphism, coral accent, single strong CTA principle
Inspired by Star Trek: Discovery title sequence (Prologue studio): sepia-soaked parchment, Leonardo da Vinci drafting notebook, blueprint logic
Surfaces feel like folios and vellum overlays placed on a drafting table — not boxed dashboard cards
Accents behave like annotation inks (marking, not painting); active states are marked not flood-filled
Corner radii: medium-small (10–18px), premium, ergonomic — never square, never overly soft
Linework is a first-class design tool: hairlines, contour rings, technical dividers, annotation marks
Glassmorphism is a dark-mode-only material — disabled in light mode
lookBooks: Runtime canvas: textured ivory parchment field with subtle grain and tonal variation
Standard cards: warm vellum folio — tonal lift, hairline border, metadata as annotations
Active guide cards: contour emphasis + edge trace + notation mark (not loud fills)
Drawers: vellum overlay — translucent warm surface, stronger edge, layered reveal
Modals: elevated folio — generous whitespace, quiet definition, warm scrim
Trust indicators: calibrated ring geometry, notched marks, technical secondary type
Smart menu active: composed drafting/input surface with one restrained accent cue
Analysis/matrix views: disciplined diagrammatic surfaces — more visible grid logic, still elegant
Empty states: faint diagram fragments + coordinate marks + editorial prompting
Loading states: structure-first reveal — contour → surface → content (see animation backlog)

who.audience: Experience creators, cartridge operators, and metaMe platform users

delivery: Real-time runtime preview in parchment intelligence environment
mechanics: Composer interface with Design & Style Guides in Planning & Parity modal
Live runtime preview with light/dark theme switching via ?theme= param
Component surfaces defined by folio/vellum material hierarchy
Trust indicators with calibrated ring geometry

differentiators: Parchment intelligence light mode — archival, warm, quietly cinematic
Star Trek: Discovery title-sequence visual grammar translated into product UI
Material consistency across shell and runtime iframe
Linework as a primary intelligence-communication device
innovations: CSS-layer light mode activation via .mm-light class — zero functional code change
Three-layer material system: parchment canvas → vellum surfaces → ink notation
Annotation-ink accent philosophy — marking not painting
Deferred animation system with backlog for structure-first reveal
emotionalImpact: Archival intelligence — the system knows what it is doing
Composed calm — intelligence being drafted and revealed
Quiet cinematic — awe through subtlety, not spectacle

success: Shell-to-runtime visual continuity (no visible material drift across iframe boundary)
Light mode adoption in runtime embeds
Creator satisfaction with Studio visual environment
kpis: UI parity review score (per ui-parity-reviewer agent)
measurement: Visual regression testing against parchment acceptance criteria

smartTriadFormatting.cssSystem: metaMe Parchment Intelligence v2.0 — warm ivory canvas (#F1EBDD), vellum surfaces (#F7F2E8), warm charcoal ink (#2E2923), annotation-ink accents (runtime #4F8C98, codex #A27A3D, make #935872), medium-small ergonomic radii (14px default), hairline borders rgba(68,57,41,0.14), warm shadows rgba(57,42,24,0.05-0.09), glassmorphism disabled in light mode
smartTriadFormatting.systemPrompt: metaMe creation guide: archival intelligence, precision draftsmanship, warm restraint, quietly cinematic. Speak with the calm confidence of a system that maps, annotates, and discovers — not a dashboard that displays.
